content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff relative to `origin/master`, read the touched execution and panel files, and spot-checked targeted tests. I found one correctness gap: stdout-only quota/usage-limit failures are still misclassified as retryable transport errors in the direct and tmux adapters.

## Amendments

1. Treat plain-text stdout exhaustion messages as `BackendExhausted` in both direct and tmux execution paths. In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1448-1456` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1490-1500`, and the mirrored tmux logic at `src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-645`, the new classifier explicitly refuses to fall back to raw stdout when `extract_stdout_error()` returns `None`. That leaves any backend that prints `usage limit`/`quota exceeded` only to stdout classified as `TransportFailure`, so it will still retry instead of degrading. Pass a bounded plain-text stdout tail into `classify_exit_failure_with_output()` when there is no structured stdout error, and add a regression test for a stdout-only exhaustion message.
   Rationale: The feature requirement was to detect persistent exhaustion from stderr/stdout. The current implementation knowingly skips the stdout-only case, so some real quota/credit failures will still burn retry budget and can still fail the run instead of gracefully dropping the exhausted backend.
